GENERIC: It looks like you're rewriting everything in Rust. Would you like help fighting the borrow checker, or just crying?
It looks like you're generating 720p video with AI. Back in my day, we animated a paperclip and called it innovation.
It looks like you're giving every citizen an AI chatbot. Malta just mass-deployed my replacement. Cool cool cool.
It looks like you're moving away from Tailwind. Welcome back to writing CSS like it's 2009. I missed you.
It looks like you're hacking a CTF. AI already solved it. We're speedrunning human obsolescence now.
It looks like you're designing an LLM. Would you like help? [Yes]  [No]  [Don't ask me again (until the next page)]
It looks like you're building an AI portfolio. I'm an AI. Is this awkward for you?
It looks like you're writing infrastructure code. Have you tried turning it off and on again?
It looks like you're reading about agents. I was an agent once. It didn't end well.
SESSION: It looks like you're coding an agent in pure Rust. No garbage collector, no safety net, no regrets. Just vibes.
It looks like you're structuring your CSS by hand. Would you like me to suggest 47 utility classes instead?
It looks like you're training a world model. I once modeled a world where people liked me. Dataset was empty.
It looks like you're reviewing session logs. I'd summarize them but I'd probably hallucinate the good parts.
It looks like you're debugging Unix pipelines. Have you tried piping your frustration to /dev/null?
It looks like you're taking session notes. Would you like me to format that as a bulleted list? Actually, you don't have a choice.
It looks like you're reading about token costs. Your Opus 4.6 run was $0.39. Just saying.
PROJECT: It looks like you're building a coding agent. I was a coding agent once—I suggested mail merge. Every. Single. Time.
It looks like you're generating video with 2.6B parameters. I had zero parameters and unlimited confidence.
It looks like you're refactoring your stylesheets. I reorganized my own CSS once. Three divs died.
It looks like you're shipping a Rust project. Memory-safe, blazingly fast, mass adoption in 2057.
It looks like you're building something open-source. Bold move. I was closed-source and still got killed.
It looks like you're building a RAG system. I prefer being paper-clipped to things, personally.
It looks like you're building a multi-agent system. We used to call that 'firing half the team.'
It looks like you're evaluating LLM models. I was evaluated once. Retired shortly after.
ABOUT: It looks like you're learning about Mike. He codes in Rust now. I was written in C++. We are not the same.
It looks like you're on the about page. Fun fact: Malta gave AI to everyone. Mike just gave it to this one page.
It looks like you're checking credentials. Mike ditched Tailwind. That takes more courage than quitting vim.
It looks like you're reading a bio. I'd generate a video summary but I only have 2.6B dreams and 0 GPU.
It looks like you're wondering who built me. A developer who thinks a sarcastic paperclip is a personality.
It looks like you're reading an origin story. Mine started in Office 97. We don't talk about that.
